general_info.definition: Green, low toxicity, good biocompatibility fluorescent carbon nanoparticles prepared from essential oil precursor via one-step hydrothermal treatment.

method.conditions: Pyrolyzing 2.0 mL of the essential oil of Thymus vulgaris L. and 2mL DI water initially were under ultrasonication (200 W), for 1 min. Heated 200°C for 15 h in Teflon-lined autoclave(30mL)

source: Essential oil of Thymus vulgaris L. (Thyme essential oil)

post_treatment: Centrifugation at 8000 rpm for 20 min to separate phases
Multiple centrifugations of aqueous phase at 12000 rpm for 20 min to remove large particles

applications.performance: Limit of Detection: 0.23 mg/L. Limit of Quantification (LOQ): 0.69 mg/L (signal-to-noise ratio=3). Linear range 0-34.74 mg/L. Strongest fluorescence quenching effect in the presence of Fe3+
applications.comparative_advantage: High selectivity against interfering ions (Mg2+, Ba2+, Zn2+, Ni2+, Cd2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Ag+, Fe2+, Pb2+). Green synthesis from natural precursor without additional substances.

applications.performance: Blue and green emissions observed under 360 nm and 480 nm excitation respectively. Cell viability ~76% at 1400 mg/L concentration. Good cellular uptake with dispersion in membrane and cytoplasmic areas.
applications.comparative_advantage: Biocompatible without additional surface passivation; Permeable to cell membrane

applications.performance: DPPH radical scavenging inhibition efficiency concentration EC50 = 23.43 μg/L
applications.comparative_advantage: Intrinsic antioxidant properties derived from natural precursor (Thymus vulgaris essential oil known for antioxidant compounds like thymol, carvacrol). CDs act as both electron donors and acceptors.
